skill.name: large-report-editor
skill.description: Edit and compile large Markdown research reports with chunked changes, structure files, backups, and citation-aware review.
skill.summary: Guides safe editing of large Markdown reports with structure tracking and citation discipline.
skill.author: ACSKamloops

security_audit.summary: The three external command findings are false positives caused by Markdown inline code around file paths on lines 7, 8, and 10. The weak cryptography finding is also a false positive because line 3 uses descriptive report-editing language, not a cryptographic algorithm. The skill does involve local file editing workflows, so it carries normal filesystem caution but no confirmed malicious behavior.

low_findings.title: False Positive: Markdown Inline Paths Flagged As Commands
low_findings.description: Lines 7, 8, and 10 use Markdown inline code to reference files and folders. They do not contain Ruby backtick execution, shell execution, or user-controlled command construction.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: The flagged text is enclosed in Markdown inline code spans and names local documentation paths. No executable syntax or command invocation appears in the file.

low_findings.title: False Positive: Descriptive Text Flagged As Weak Cryptography
low_findings.description: Line 3 contains the phrase legal-grade citation rules in the skill description. It does not reference MD5, SHA-1, DES, RC4, or any weak cryptographic algorithm.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: The finding appears to match descriptive wording rather than code or configuration. The reviewed file has no cryptographic operations.

content.value_statement: Large reports are difficult to edit without losing structure or citations. This skill guides chunked Markdown edits, backups, and citation-aware review.
actual_capabilities: Guides edits for very large Markdown research reports.
Uses structure files to preserve report organization during changes.
Requires citations for newly added factual claims.
Helps compile report sections after targeted changes.
limitations: Does not verify sources unless the user provides source material.
Does not replace legal or academic review.
Requires access to the report files and referenced instructions.
Cannot guarantee citation accuracy without page-level evidence.

use_cases.title: Revise Long Research Drafts
use_cases.description: Update a large Markdown report while keeping headings, citations, and section order consistent.

use_cases.title: Prepare Evidence-Based Reports
use_cases.description: Add new findings only when they include stable citations and page anchors.

use_cases.title: Compile Document Deliverables
use_cases.description: Merge edited sections into a coherent report after staged review.

prompt_templates.title: Review Report Structure
prompt_templates.prompt: Review the report structure and identify sections that need editing. Do not change files yet.

prompt_templates.title: Edit One Section
prompt_templates.prompt: Edit the named Markdown section using the provided evidence. Preserve existing citations and add page anchors for new claims.

prompt_templates.title: Apply Chunked Revisions
prompt_templates.prompt: Revise the report in small chunks. Create a backup first, then summarize each changed section and citation impact.

prompt_templates.title: Compile Final Report
prompt_templates.prompt: Compile the edited sections into the final report. Check structure consistency, unresolved citations, and factual claims before finishing.

output_examples.input: Update the findings section with three cited points from the provided source packet.
output_examples.output: The findings section is revised with three sourced claims, preserved headings, and citation notes for each added point.

output_examples.input: Check the report for uncited new claims after revisions.
output: Uncited factual claims are listed by section.
Existing citation gaps are separated from new revision issues.
Recommended fixes reference the needed source evidence.

output_examples.input: Prepare the final compiled Markdown report from approved sections.
output_examples.output: The compiled report keeps the approved section order, includes citation checks, and notes any unresolved evidence gaps.

best_practices: Create a backup before major document edits.
Edit one logical section at a time for easier review.
Require stable citations for every new factual claim.
anti_patterns: Do not add facts without source evidence.
Do not rewrite the entire report when a targeted patch is enough.
Do not ignore local report instructions or structure files.

faq.question: What type of reports does this skill support?
faq.answer: It supports large Markdown research reports that need structured edits, compilation, and citation checks.

faq.question: Can it verify every citation automatically?
faq.answer: No. It can enforce citation discipline, but source verification depends on the evidence provided.

faq.question: Does it create backups?
faq.answer: The skill instructs the assistant to use backups before major edits when the workspace supports them.

faq.question: Is it safe for confidential reports?
faq.answer: Use normal workspace controls. The skill does not request network transfer, but it edits local report files.

faq.question: Can it work with Claude, Codex, and Claude Code?
faq.answer: Yes. The metadata lists Claude, Codex, and Claude Code as supported tools.

faq.question: What should users provide before editing?
faq.answer: Provide the report files, local editing instructions, source evidence, and the exact sections to revise.
